originalTitle: Four sentenced to death for killing worshippers at Catholic church in Nigeria
title: Making Things Right: News from Our Courts in Nigeria
content:
Hello everyone! It's your friendly reporter, Pendo, here to tell you some important news from our big continent, Africa! Today, we're talking about something that happened in a country called Nigeria. It's about how grown-ups work together to make sure everyone is safe and that good things happen when bad things try to sneak in. Sometimes, sad things happen, but then brave people, like judges in a court, help to make things right again. Let's learn about it together!

A Sad Day Becomes a Day for Justice

A little while ago, something very, very sad happened in a church in Nigeria. Churches are special places where people go to pray, sing, and feel happy and safe together. They are places for families and friends to meet and feel peace. But one day, some grown-ups came and hurt people there. It made many, many people feel scared and sad in their hearts. When something like this happens, it's like a big puzzle that needs to be solved. Who did it? Why did they do it? And how can we make sure it doesn't happen again, so everyone can feel safe once more? That's where special grown-ups called judges and lawyers come in. They work very hard to find answers and make sure that people who do bad things learn a lesson, and that everyone else can feel safe again. This important work is called finding 'justice'. Justice means making things fair and right for everyone. *Did you know?* Nigeria is a very big country in West Africa! It has many different languages and cultures, just like a beautiful rainbow! People there love to tell stories and sing songs.

What is a Court?

Imagine a special room, like a very important classroom for grown-ups, but even more serious! This room is called a court. In a court, a very smart grown-up called a judge helps to listen to stories and look at clues. It's like being a super detective, but with lots of rules! When something wrong happens, like someone getting hurt, the police try their very best to find out who did it. They talk to people, look at places, and gather information. Then, they bring all their clues and stories to the court. Other grown-ups, called lawyers, help tell the stories for different people involved. It's all about finding the truth, just like when you try to figure out who ate the last biscuit! The court is there to make sure everyone is treated fairly and that the rules of the country are followed. These rules are called laws, and they help keep everyone safe and make sure our communities live in peace. *Did you know?* Judges wear special robes, sometimes black, to show that they are very important and fair. It's like a superhero's uniform for justice, showing that they are there to help!

Looking for Clues and Truth

In this sad story from Nigeria, the police worked very, very hard. They looked for many clues, like footprints or things left behind, and they talked to many, many people who might have seen something. They found five men who they thought might have done the bad thing at the church. So, these five men went to the court. Going to court doesn't mean you are bad right away; it means the court is trying to find out what really happened. Everyone gets a chance to tell their side of the story, and grown-ups listen very carefully. The judge listens to all the clues and all the stories from everyone. This careful looking and listening is called looking at "evidence." Evidence is like tiny pieces of a big puzzle that help the judge see the whole picture and understand what is true. *Did you know?* It's very important to tell the truth in court. Everyone tries their best to find out exactly what happened, so telling the truth helps a lot!

What the Judge Found Out

After listening for a long, long time, for many days and weeks, and looking at all the clues and evidence, the judge made a very important decision. Out of the five men, the judge found that four of them were indeed responsible for the sad things that happened at the church. This means they were found "guilty." Being guilty means the court found enough strong proof, like all the puzzle pieces fitting together, to say they did something wrong and hurt people. It's a very serious word for doing something very, very bad. But what about the fifth man? For him, the judge looked at all the clues and listened to all the stories, and then said, "There is not enough proof to say this man did the wrong thing." So, this man was found "not guilty." Being not guilty means the court does not have enough strong evidence to say you did something bad. So, he was allowed to go free. This shows us that the court is very careful and fair. It only punishes people when there is strong, strong proof that they did something wrong. It is important to be fair to everyone.

A Very Serious Decision

For the four men who were found guilty, because they hurt so many people in a special, peaceful place like a church, the judge made a very serious decision. The court said they would face the biggest, most serious punishment allowed by the law for such a terrible act. This is for when people do something very, very wrong and hurt a whole community deeply. It's the law's way of saying that hurting people like this is never, ever okay, and it helps to protect everyone else by showing how serious such actions are. This very serious punishment is called being "sentenced." It means the judge officially says what will happen to them because of what they did. This decision sends a message that such actions will not be ignored, and justice will be sought. *Did you know?* Every country has its own special rules called laws to help people live together safely and peacefully. Laws are like guidebooks for everyone to follow!

Why Is This News Important?

This news is very important because it shows us that even when very sad and bad things happen, there are grown-ups who work tirelessly to make things right. It shows us that courts and judges are there to help our communities stay safe and sound. When someone does something wrong, the court helps find out who it was and makes sure they face the consequences for their actions. This helps everyone else feel safer and know that bad actions have serious results. It helps bring peace back to the community. It also reminds all of us that churches, mosques, temples, and other special places for prayer are meant to be safe, peaceful, and full of kindness. Everyone should feel safe and welcome when they are there, no matter their religion or beliefs. This decision from the court helps to tell everyone that these special places must always be safe and respected.

Learning About Fairness and Safety

This story from Nigeria helps us learn about fairness. The court was fair because it listened to everyone's stories and looked at all the clues very carefully. It was fair because it found one person not guilty when there wasn't enough proof to say he did wrong. And it was fair because it gave a very serious punishment to those who were proven guilty of doing a very bad thing. It shows that the law treats everyone fairly. Safety is super important for all of us. We all want to feel safe at home with our families, at school with our friends and teachers, and in our communities when we play outside. When grown-ups in the court do their job so carefully and bravely, they are helping to build a safer world for all of us, especially for children like you. They help make sure that when someone breaks the rules and hurts others, they will be held accountable for their actions. *Did you know?* Everyone, even children, can help make their community safer by being kind to others, reporting bullying, and always telling a trusted grown-up if they see something wrong or feel unsafe!

What We Can Do

As children, we can learn many important things from this news. We can learn to be kind to each other, to help our friends when they need it, and to always listen to our grown-ups who teach us good things. We can also learn that if we ever see something that makes us feel unsafe, or if we are worried about something, we should always tell a grown-up we trust, like a parent, a teacher, a grandparent, or an auntie or uncle. They are there to help us and keep us safe. This story also reminds us to respect all special places where people go to pray, no matter what their religion is. These are places for peace, for learning, and for kindness. We should always treat them with respect. So, while it was a sad start with a bad event, this news shows us that justice can be found. It shows that courts are working hard to make our African communities safe and fair for everyone. Thank you for listening, and remember to always be kind, be safe, and help each other!
summary: In Nigeria, a court decided that four men who hurt people at a church were guilty and will face a very serious punishment, while one man was found not guilty because there was not enough proof.
whyMatters: This news is important because it shows that grown-ups called judges work hard to find truth and make sure people who do bad things face consequences, helping everyone in our communities feel safer and reminding us that special places like churches should always be peaceful.

originalTitle: Shell pumped oil through Nigeria pipeline for years despite pollution evidence, documents show
title: Oil Spills and Our Earth: A Story from Nigeria!
content:
Hello, amazing Earth helpers! I'm your super kid-friendly news reporter, right here in beautiful Africa! Today, we have an important story from a lovely country called Nigeria. It's about something called 'oil' and why we need to be extra careful with it to keep our Earth healthy and happy!

Meet Our Friend: Oil!

Oil is like a very special, thick liquid that comes from deep, deep under the ground. It's been there for millions of years! It's super important for many things we use every day. Think about your family's car – it needs oil to go! Airplanes fly with oil, and even some of our toys and plastics are made from oil. It's truly amazing stuff! Did you know? People sometimes call oil “black gold” because it's so valuable and helps us do so many things!

What is a Pipeline?

Now, how does this oil get from deep underground to our cars and toys? Well, big companies use something called a 'pipeline.' Imagine a very, very long, giant straw, or a super-long tube! These pipelines carry the oil safely from one place to another, sometimes for hundreds of kilometers! It's a clever way to move lots of oil across big lands and even under the sea. Did you know? Some pipelines are longer than many, many cities put together! They are built to be very strong to hold the oil inside.

A Story from Nigeria

Our story takes us to Nigeria, a wonderful country in West Africa with lots of green forests, sunny beaches, and busy cities. For many years, a big oil company called Shell had these pipelines there. Their job was to take the oil from under the ground and move it. But sometimes, sad things happen. The documents – which are like important papers – showed that oil was pumping, even when there were signs that oil was leaking or spilling out of the pipelines. Imagine if your water bottle had a tiny hole, and water kept dripping out, making a mess. That’s a bit like what happened, but with oil! Did you know? Nigeria is home to many different kinds of animals, including elephants, lions, and lots of colorful birds! It's so important to protect their homes.

What is Pollution?

When oil leaks out, it makes a big mess. This mess is called 'pollution.' Pollution happens when bad things get into our clean environment – like our air, water, or land – and make them dirty or unhealthy. Oil pollution is very serious because oil is thick, sticky, and can be poisonous. When oil spills, it doesn't just look bad; it makes everything dirty and smelly. It spreads like a giant, dark blanket over the water and land. It's like someone spilled a big pot of black, gooey paint all over your clean floor, but much, much worse for nature! Did you know? Even a little bit of oil can make a very big mess because it spreads out so much!

How Did It Hurt Nature?

Oh, dear! When oil spilled from the pipelines in Nigeria, it hurt so many parts of nature. Imagine a beautiful river where fish swim and birds fly. When oil gets into the river, it makes the water dirty. Fish find it hard to breathe and swim, and they can get sick. Some might even die. This is very sad for our fishy friends. Birds that fly over the water sometimes land in the oil. Their beautiful feathers get all sticky and heavy. They can't fly anymore, and they can't keep themselves warm. It's like having glue all over your body! Also, the plants and trees on the land near the spills got covered in oil too. They couldn't grow properly, and the soil became very unhealthy. The animals that eat these plants couldn't find good food. Did you know? Some oil spills can take many, many years to clean up, and sometimes the damage stays for a very long time.

How Did It Hurt People?

It's not just animals and plants that got hurt. The people living near these oil spills were also in great trouble. Many families in Nigeria get their food and make their living from fishing in the rivers or farming on the land. When the water was full of oil, they couldn't catch fish to eat or to sell at the market. So, they lost their food and their money. And when the land was covered in oil, they couldn't grow healthy crops like maize or vegetables. Their farms became sick. The air near the spills could also become smelly and unhealthy to breathe, making children and grown-ups sick. Their homes and villages became dirty and difficult to live in. It's so important that companies take care of the places where people live. Did you know? Many people in Nigeria live in small villages and rely on the land and rivers for almost everything they need.

What Did Shell Say?

When people looked at the old papers and asked about the spills, the big oil company, Shell, said something important. They said that the papers don't tell the whole story. They said it was a very 'complex operating environment' at that time. What they meant is that it was a very hard and tricky place to work, with many challenges. Maybe there were many things making it difficult to keep the pipelines safe and clean up spills right away. They say the situation was not simple. But still, the papers showed that they knew about the pollution happening for years. It reminds us that even when things are difficult, it's super important to try our best to keep our Earth safe and clean. Did you know? Being responsible means that when something goes wrong, you try to understand why and then make things better!

What Can We Learn?

This story from Nigeria teaches us a very important lesson: We all need to be Earth's best friends! It shows us how important it is for big companies and all of us to be very careful with our environment. We must always try to prevent pollution, and if something does spill or make a mess, we should clean it up as fast as possible. Listening to signs of trouble and taking quick action can save so many animals, plants, and even help people stay healthy and happy. Our Earth is a precious home for everyone. Did you know? We only have one Earth, and it's up to all of us, big and small, to take care of it!

Be a Super Earth Helper!

So, what can YOU do to be a super Earth helper? You can help by not littering, by turning off lights when you leave a room to save energy, and by telling grown-ups if you see something that looks like pollution. Every little bit helps our amazing planet. Let's work together to keep Africa and the whole world sparkling clean and healthy for all living things! Thank you for listening, my wonderful Earth helpers! Keep being curious and kind to our world!
summary: Old papers showed that Shell, a big oil company in Nigeria, kept pumping oil through pipelines for many years, even when there was evidence of pollution, hurting nature and people, but Shell says it was a very challenging time.
whyMatters: This story matters because it teaches us how important it is to protect our Earth from pollution, especially from big spills like oil, so animals, plants, and people can stay healthy and safe.

originalTitle: Elon Musk’s SpaceX eyes $1.77tn valuation ahead of historic IPO
title: SpaceX: Reaching for the Stars with Lots of Money!
content:
Hello from Africa! Let's Talk Space Rockets!

Hello, my wonderful friends! It's me, your friendly news reporter, here to share some super exciting news from far, far away, about rockets and space! Can you imagine rockets flying up, up, up into the sky, past the clouds, and even beyond to where the stars twinkle? Well, that's what we're going to talk about today!

Meet SpaceX and Elon Musk!

There's a super cool company called **SpaceX**. They are like super builders, but instead of houses or cars, they build amazing rockets! These rockets are very, very big, like a tall building, and they help us explore space. The person who started SpaceX is a very smart man named Elon Musk. He dreams big, bigger than most people, and wants to help us go to other planets! SpaceX builds these big rockets in a place called Texas, far away in America. They don't just build them; they launch them into space! Can you imagine seeing a rocket launch? It's like a giant firework show, but even more powerful, taking brave astronauts and important satellites all the way to space.

What is a 'Company' and Why Do They Need Money?

Now, what exactly is a "company"? Think of a company like a big team of people who work together to make something special. Maybe your favourite toy company makes amazing toys, or a company makes yummy snacks. SpaceX is a company that makes rockets and sends them to space. To do all this amazing work, companies need something very important: money! Money helps them buy special materials to build rockets, pay the clever scientists and engineers who design them, and fuel the rockets for their big journey. Without money, it would be hard to build even one rocket, let alone many!

Slices of a Giant Space Cake! (What are Shares?)

So, SpaceX needs more money to keep building rockets and exploring space. How do they get it? Imagine SpaceX is baking a giant, delicious space cake! Instead of selling the whole cake to just one person, they decide to cut it into many, many tiny slices. Each slice is like a little piece of the company. These tiny pieces are called "shares." SpaceX is thinking about selling these slices, or shares, for the very first time to grown-ups who want to help the company. This special moment is called an "IPO," but that's a big grown-up word. For us, it just means they are sharing pieces of their company. They want to sell each slice for about $135. That's a lot of money, like buying a super cool bicycle, but for grown-ups who love space, it's a chance to own a tiny piece of a rocket company! They hope to get a *lot* of money, around 75 billion dollars! Can you even imagine that much money? It's enough to build many, many schools and hospitals, or buy all the toys in many shops! This big amount of money will help SpaceX do even more incredible things in space. **Did you know?** When a company sells its shares for the very first time, it's a very big and important day for them!

So Much Money! What Will SpaceX Do?

With all this new money, SpaceX has some super exciting plans! * **Building More Rockets:** They can build even more powerful rockets to go further and faster than ever before. These new rockets will be like super-fast space buses! * **Sending Satellites:** They can send more satellites into space. Satellites are like clever eyes in the sky. They help us watch the weather, make our phones work, and even give us internet so we can learn and play online here in Africa! * **Sending Astronauts:** SpaceX already helps send brave astronauts to the International Space Station, which is like a giant house floating in space where scientists live and work. With more money, they can send even more people there! * **Going to Mars!** This is one of Elon Musk's biggest dreams! He wants to send people to the planet Mars. Mars is a red planet, and it would be amazing to explore it. More money means they can build the special spacecraft needed for such a long and exciting journey.

Rockets That Come Back Home!

One of the coolest things about SpaceX rockets is that some of them can come back down to Earth and land safely! Other rockets usually just fall into the ocean after they launch. But SpaceX rockets are special because they can be used again, just like you can reuse a school bag or a water bottle. **Did you know?** Reusing rockets helps save a lot of money and is better for our Earth because it means less waste! SpaceX was the first company to successfully land and reuse an orbital rocket. Isn't that clever?

How Space Helps Us Here in Africa!

Even though SpaceX is far away, what they do in space helps us a lot right here in Africa! Those satellites they send up? They help bring faster internet to our homes and schools, connect us with friends and family far away, and even help farmers know when the rain is coming. This helps us grow food and stay safe. So, when SpaceX gets more money to send more satellites, it means better connections and more opportunities for all of us!

Dreaming Big for Our Future!

So, SpaceX is getting ready to do something very big to get more money. This money will help them build even more amazing rockets, send people to space, explore Mars, and make our world more connected through satellites. It's all about pushing the boundaries and dreaming big, big, big! Who knows, maybe one day, some of you will be the ones building rockets or flying to space! Keep dreaming and learning, my friends! **Did you know?** Elon Musk also started the company Tesla, which makes electric cars that don't need petrol!
summary: SpaceX, a rocket company led by Elon Musk, plans to sell small pieces of its company (called shares) to get a lot of money, around $75 billion, to build more rockets, send satellites and astronauts to space, and explore places like Mars.
whyMatters: This is important because with more money, SpaceX can build better rockets, send more things to space like internet satellites that help us here in Africa, and even take people to other planets, which helps us learn new things about the universe and discover amazing possibilities for our future.
